These large waves made ocean activities dangerous, as well as created strong currents and beach runup.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Surf heights up to 26 feet created strong currents, beach run up, and made navigating channels dangerous.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low that passed a few hundred miles north of the state produced gale force winds and heavy rainfall over all of the main Hawaiian Islands. The worst flooding affected O'ahu during the daylight hours of January 30 and southwest Maui that night. The Kuli'ou'ou and Hawai'i Kai areas appeared to have the worst flooding on O'ahu due to the passage of an intense rain band with 2- to 4-inch per hour rain rates. On Maui, the southwestern flank of Haleakala received 3 to 6 inches of rain, and the southeastern side had 3 to 11 inches. Flooding caused considerable damage in Kihei as several normally dry gulches overflowed. Most of South Kihei Road was closed due to the flooding, and numerous businesses and residences were impacted. The main rain band shifted to the Big Island during the early morning hours of January 31. Most of the rainfall occurred over the western half of the island, with widespread totals of 2 to 5 inches but no reports of significant flood damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: South Kihei Road was closed at multiple locations due to flooding. Numerous businesses and residences in Kihei were impacted by the flooding as several gulches became overwhelmed and overflowed their banks.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mesoscale convective complex formed within an upper level trough axis centered over the eastern half of the state. Severe thunderstorms developed east of this trough axis and moved south onto the island of Maui.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees down near mile marker 26 and mile marker 27 along Nahiku Road.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees down in the vicinity of Rice Park.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: While much of the state received above average rainfall in January, extreme drought conditions remained in some areas due to the low rainfall from the previous months. The portions included southwest Kauai, west Molokai, southwest Maui, and west and upslope Big Island.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The USDA on Kauai reported that a producer operating in the southeast side of the island may need to reduce his herd size due to lack of feed. Ranchers have been continuing to provide supplemental feed.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A parked plane was blown into another parked plane. The nose of one plane pierced the bottom of a plane parked perpendicular to it. Damage cost was estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Below average rainfall trends have allowed severe to extreme drought conditions to persist over the interior and upslope as well as the southeastern areas of the Big Island.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The College of Tropical Agriculture and Human Resilience���s (CETAHR) Cooperative Extension reported poor range conditions across the entire Big Island, providing marginal and supplemental feed. They also reported grasses drying up and slow productivity. Ranchers operating in the Hamakua district of the Big Island reported poor pasture conditions, supplemental feeding requirements, and less water in ponds and creeks. Ranchers were also weaning some of the cattle early, as well as destocking cows due to lack of available pasture feed. A mandatory 25 percent water restriction in the Hakalau area of the Big Island went into effect.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Below average rainfall allowed severe drought conditions to continue over the western and southern portions of Maui.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Mandatory non-potable water conservation efforts were still in place for West Maui with irrigation disallowed.

A large long period south swell brought large surf and dangerous conditions to south facing shores of each island.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A seventy five foot passenger vessel called the Atlantis discovery ran aground on shallow reef near Kewalo Basin in Honolulu. This happened after the boat got caught by a set of large waves of ten to fourteen feet.  A high surf advisory was in place during that time.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large long period south swell brought large surf and dangerous conditions to south facing shores of each island.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High surf of ten to fourteen feet combined with higher than normal tides to cause coastal flooding on Kihei Road. Officials closed the southbound lanes between Uwapo and Ohukai Roads for repairs.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large surf and higher then normal tides flooded Ala Moana beach park and Magic Island.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A brushfire broke out in Kunia and burned over six hundred acres.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wildfire broke out near Kunia camp and Kunia Road on August 19 and was found to have been started intentionally by the Honolulu Fire Department. Firefighters fought the fire over the course of several days and by August 25, the fire was considered one hundred percent contained. The fire destroyed nearly six hundred acres of wildland near agricultural areas.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Below average rainfall allowed severe to extreme drought conditions to continue over much of the Big Island, especially over the Hamakua coast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The College of Tropical Agriculture and Human Resilience's Cooperative Extension reported poor range conditions across the Big Island, with ranchers providing marginal feed with supplemental feeding. There were also persistent water shortages and ranchers have reportedly had to haul water at a great expense. Ranchers were weaning some cattle early, and destocking cows due to lack of available pasture feed. Many ranchers reported not seeing conditions similar to this since near 2008. There were reportedly twenty five percent water restrictions in place in the Hakalau area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very little rainfall over the past few months have allowed severe drought conditions to continue over Molokai and most of Maui County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was little to no rainfall reported for most of Maui County, except localized windward areas. A stage one water shortage remained in effect for Upcountry, West, South, and Central Maui since September of 2024.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A wildfire broke out in Maili on July 7 and burnt about ninety five acres. Two homes were damaged in the fire and one fatality was reported. Moderate drought conditions were in place at the time of the fire.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: According to KITV, Honolulu fire Department received a call at 2:33pm for a wildfire near Paakea road in Maili. A total of twenty nine Honolulu fire Department units along with units from the Federal Fire Department worked to contain the fire. The fire was ninety percent contained at 1:30 on Monday morning, and burnt about ninety five acres. A total of two homes were damaged in the fire as well as an unconfirmed number of other small structures.  According to mayor Rick Blangiardi, a ninety four year old woman had died. She was taken in critical conditions by EMS due to smoke inhalation and second-degree burns.

There were also persistent water shortages and ranchers have reportedly had to haul water at a great expense. Many ranchers reported not seeing conditions similar to this since near 2008. There were reportedly twenty five percent water restrictions in place in the Hakalau area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large 8.8 magnitude earthquake occurred in Kamchatka Russia and sent a tsunami across the Pacific to the Hawaiian islands.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 4.9 foot tsunami generated from an 8.8 magnitude earthquake in Russia flooded the SCP Hilo Hotel. Water inundated the Hotel's lower levels, including the parking area, driveway, and approximately ten rooms. Guests were evacuated beforehand to shelters.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A mid level trough combined with an upper level low and enhanced moisture allowed significant flooding to occur over portions of Kauai. Mt Wai'ale'ale recorded nearly eight inches of rainfall in a twenty four hour period.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Kauai Emergency Management Agency reported that a large amount of water inundated Kuhio Highway at the Hanalei Bridge. Flooding was also reported near the mouth of Wailua River.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large long period south swell brought large surf and dangerous conditions to south facing shores of each island.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High and choppy surf caused a fifteen foot long boat to capsize off the Kapolei coast. As a result, a sixty six year old man fell overboard and went into cardiac arrest. He was transported by emergency medical services to the emergency room and later pronounced dead. There were two other people on board the vessel at the time of capsizing.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Months of below average rainfall have led to continuing severe drought conditions over portions of Molokai, particularly the western half.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Molokai reservoir levels were lower than last year at this time.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Months of below average rainfall have led to continuing severe drought conditions over portions of Molokai, particularly the western half.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Molokai reservoir levels were lower than last year at this time.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Months of below average rainfall have led to continuing extreme drought conditions over portions of the Big Island. Particularly the upper Hamakua coast and southeast portions of the Big Island experienced the worst drought conditions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Continued water deliveries were needed across the Big Island to catchment customers.  Also poor range conditions across the Big Island persisted, with supplemental feeding required and water shortages, especially in Hamakua and Kau Districts. Ranchers weaned cattle early and destocked cows due to lack of available pasture feed, as well as moved cattle to the Kona District for better grazing conditions. Many reports cited not seeing conditions like this since around 2008. Several brush fires were reported in the Hamakua District. There were also ten percent water reduction/conservation recommendations for Hamakua, South Kohala, and South Hilo Districts of the Big Island.

Ranchers weaned cattle early and destocked cows due to lack of available pasture feed, as well as moved cattle to the Kona District for better grazing conditions. Many reports cited not seeing conditions like this since around 2008. Several brush fires were reported in the Hamakua District. There were also ten percent water reduction/conservation recommendations for Hamakua, South Kohala, and South Hilo Districts of the Big Island.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Months of below average rainfall have led to continuing extreme drought conditions over portions of the Big Island. Particularly the upper Hamakua coast and southeast portions of the Big Island experienced the worst drought conditions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Continued water deliveries were needed across the Big Island to catchment customers.  Also poor range conditions across the Big Island persisted, with supplemental feeding required and water shortages, especially in Hamakua and Kau Districts. Ranchers weaned cattle early and destocked cows due to lack of available pasture feed, as well as moved cattle to the Kona District for better grazing conditions. Many reports cited not seeing conditions like this since around 2008. Several brush fires were reported in the Hamakua District. There were also ten percent water reduction/conservation recommendations for Hamakua, South Kohala, and South Hilo Districts of the Big Island.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Months of below average rainfall have led to continuing severe drought conditions over portions of Maui, particularly the south and western portions.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A stage 1 water shortage continued for South and Central Maui since September 2024 due to lack of surface water flow. West Maui continued a stage 2 water shortage from 9/4/25 (Stage 1 declared 6/30/22) to (10/31/2025). Upcountry Maui briefly upgraded to a stage 3 water shortage on 10/17/25, then stepped down to Stage 2 in late October (Stage 1 on 9/12/25).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The monthly peak high tides contributed to some minor coastal flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Videos on social media appeared to show several inches to around a foot of water inundating the roads around the Mapunapuna Industrial Area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Below average rainfall allowed for severe to extreme drought conditions to persist across portions of the Big Island. The Hamakua Coast in particular was affected by the drought.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Poor range conditions were observed across the Big Island, with supplemental feeding required and persistent water shortages, especially in Hamakua and Kau districts. Ranchers reportedly weaned cattle early and de-stocked cows due to lack of available pasture feed. They also reportedly had to haul water in and move cattle to the Kona district for better grazing conditions. Many reports cite not seeing conditions like this since around 2008.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front with robust upper level support brought localized flooding impacts to Kauai and Oahu. Moderate southerly winds pushed moist air with precipitable water values in excess of 1.8 inches over the islands, contributing to periods of heavy rainfall. Instability aloft from a broad mid and upper level trough allowed for isolated thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Multiple roads reportedly had several feet of water, due to poor drainage. These roads included Richards Street, H-1 westbound under Radford Drive, and Nimitz Highway near the Likelike Highway. Additionally, there was a water rescue in Kalihi.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong trade wind fetch to the east northeast of the islands sent a large easterly swell to east facing shores of the islands. This swell brought surf heights over ten feet.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: County Emergency Management reported high surf that deposited debris on Bayfront Highway. This led to partial road closures, and the Coconut Island parking lot was closed due to ocean water inundation. Hilo Bay beaches were also closed due to ocean inundation from the high surf.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A storm force low in the northwest Pacific sent an extra large swell towards the Hawaiian islands. This extra large long period swell brought waves up to 35 to 45 feet along north facing shores of Niihau, Kauai, Oahu, and Molokai. Surf reached 20 to 30 feet along west facing shores of Niihau, Kauai, Oahu, Molokai, and north facing shores of Maui. The large waves caused ocean water to surge up beaches and inundate roads as well as residents backyards.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large breaking waves surged up the beach and deposited debris in private resident's backyards. The waves also caused water to pool on Kamehameha Highway near Rockpiles.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hot, dry, and light wind conditions allowed a dust devil to form at a local swap meet.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: High winds from a dust devil caused tents and merchandise at a swap meet to become airborne. According to news media reports, ���One by one, people���s tents were starting to come off the frame, and then the frame started to lift up as well so it was very chaotic because people���s merchandise was flying everywhere,��� one witness said, ���people���s cars were getting smashed, people were getting hit in the head by poles.��� Emergency Medical Services reportedly treated a twenty eight year old woman for injuries after she was hit in the head by a tent that became airborne. She was reportedly taken to the hospital in serious condition.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Below average rainfall allowed drought conditions to persist across mainly southern and western areas of Maui. The Department of Land and Natural Resources reported Maui stream levels were the lowest seen in over a century. In particular, the W. Wailuaiki Stream was the lowest in early October in 106-year history.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: West Maui reportedly had a stage two water shortage beginning September 4 and lasted through the month.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A brush fire was reported near the Manowaialee Forest Reserve and burned near the koa plantation. The fire burned through about 420 acres, and were reportedly exacerbated by severe drought conditions. High pressure north of the islands, low humidity and little rainfall helped contribute to the spread of the fire. No structures were destroyed and no evacuations were necessary.

Instability aloft from a broad mid and upper level trough allowed for isolated thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: According to Emergency Management and the Fire Department, there was a water evacuation in Kaneohe and Ahuimanu. Additionally, there was several feet of ponded flooding on Kapaa Quarry Road near Mokapu Road.
